contentBlocks.translation: Anjaneya (son of Anjana), Mahavira (great hero), Hanuman, Marutatmaja (son of the Wind-god); Tatvajnanaprada (bestower of true knowledge), Sitadevi Mudrapradayaka (one who delivered Sita's ring as a token of Rama's message).

Ashokavanikachetta (destroyer of the Ashoka grove), Sarvamayavibhanjana (dispeller of all illusions); Sarvabandhavimokta (liberator from all bondage), Rakshovidhvamsakaraka (destroyer of the rakshasas).

Paravidyaparihara (remover of hostile learning), Parashauryavinashana (destroyer of enemy valor); Paramantranirakartta (neutralizer of hostile mantras), Parayantraprabheda (breaker of hostile yantras).

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वग्रहविनाशी च भीमसेनसहायकृत् ।
सर्वदुःखहरः सर्वलोकचारी मनोजवः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvagrahavināśī cha bhīmasēnasahāyakṛt ।
sarvaduḥkhaharaḥ sarvalōkachārī manōjavaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sarvagrahavinashi (destroyer of all malefic planetary forces), Bhimasena Sahayakrit (helper of Bhima); Sarvadukhahara (remover of all sorrows), Sarvalokachari (one who roams all worlds), Manojava (swift as thought).

Parijatadrumulastha (one who dwells at the Parijata tree's root), Sarvamantrasvarupavan (embodiment of all mantras); Sarvatantrasvarupi (embodiment of all tantras), Sarvayantritmaka (the soul of all yantras).

contentBlocks.original:
कपीश्वरो महाकायः सर्वरोगहरः प्रभुः ।
बलसिद्धिकरः सर्वविद्यासम्पत्प्रदायकः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kapīśvarō mahākāyaḥ sarvarōgaharaḥ prabhuḥ ।
balasiddhikaraḥ sarvavidyāsampatpradāyakaḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kapishhvara (lord of monkeys), Mahakaya (of immense form), Sarvarogahara (remover of all diseases), Prabhu (master); Balasiddhikara (bestower of strength and accomplishment), Sarvavidyasampatpradayaka (bestower of all knowledge and prosperity).

Kapisenanaяaka (commander of the monkey army), Bhavishyachchaturanana (the future Brahma, the four-faced creator); Kumara Brahmachari (the ever-celibate youthful one), Ratnakundaladiptiman (radiant with gem-studded earrings).

The one resplendent with his flowing, upraised tail and shining tuft of hair; Gandharvavidyatattvajña (knower of the truths of Gandharva arts and music), Mahabalaparakrama (of immense strength and boundless valor).

contentBlocks.original:
कारागृहविमोक्ता च शृङ्खलाबन्धमोचकः ।
सागरोत्तारकः प्राज्ञो रामदूतः प्रतापवान् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kārāgṛhavimōktā cha śṛṅkhalābandhamōchakaḥ ।
sāgarōttārakaḥ prājñō rāmadūtaḥ pratāpavān ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Karagrihavimokta (liberator from prison), Shrinkhalabandhramochaka (one who breaks chains of bondage); Sagarottaraka (the ocean-crosser), Prajna (supremely wise), Ramaduta (messenger of Rama), Pratapavan (glorious and mighty).
Vanara (monkey), Kesarisuta (son of Kesari), Sitashokanivaraka (remover of Sita's grief); Anjanagarbhasambhuta (born of Anjana's womb), Balarkasadrishanana (face radiant like the rising sun).

contentBlocks.original:
विभीषणप्रियकरो दशग्रीवकुलान्तकः ।
लक्ष्मणप्राणदाता च वज्रकायो महाद्युतिः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vibhīṣaṇapriyakarō daśagrīvakulāntakaḥ ।
lakṣmaṇaprāṇadātā cha vajrakāyō mahādyutiḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vibhishanapriyakara (beloved by Vibhishana), Dashagriivakulantaka (destroyer of Ravana's clan); Lakshmanaprañadatri (restorer of Lakshmana's life), Vajrakaya (body hard as thunderbolt), Mahadyuti (greatly radiant).

contentBlocks.original:
चिरञ्जीवी रामभक्तो दैत्यकार्यविघातकः ।
अक्षहन्ता काञ्चनाभः पञ्चवक्त्रो महातपाः ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chirañjīvī rāmabhaktō daityakāryavighātakaḥ ।
akṣahantā kāñchanābhaḥ pañchavaktrō mahātapāḥ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Chiranjiivi (immortal), Ramabhakta (devoted to Rama), Daityakaryavighataka (obstructer of the demons' designs); Akshahanta (slayer of Aksha), Kanchanabha (golden-hued), Panchavaktra (five-faced), Mahatapa (great ascetic).

Lankinibhanjana (destroyer of the demoness Lankini), Shriman (glorious), Simhikapranabhanjana (slayer of Simhika); Gandhamadanashailastha (dweller of Gandhamadana mountain), Lankapuravidahaka (the one who set Lanka ablaze).

contentBlocks.original:
सुग्रीवसचिवो धीरः शूरो दैत्यकुलान्तकः ।
सुरार्चितो महातेजा रामचूडामणिप्रदः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sugrīvasachivō dhīraḥ śūrō daityakulāntakaḥ ।
surārchitō mahātējā rāmachūḍāmaṇipradaḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sugrivasachiva (minister of Sugriva), Dhira (steadfast), Shura (heroic), Daityakulantaka (destroyer of the demon race); Surarchita (worshipped by the gods), Mahateja (greatly radiant), Ramachudamaniprada (one who delivered Rama's signet ring to Sita).

Kamarrupi (able to assume any form at will), Pingalaksha (reddish-brown eyed), Vardhimaìnakapujita (worshipped by Mount Mainaka in the ocean); Kabalikritamartandamandala (one who once swallowed the solar orb as a child), Vijitendriya (master of the senses).

Ramasugriivasandhata (one who forged the alliance between Rama and Sugriva), Mahiraavanamardana (crusher of Mahiravana); Sphaṭikabha (pure and crystal-clear in form), Vagadhisha (lord of speech and wisdom), Navavyakritipaṇḍita (scholar versed in the nine systems of grammar).

Chaturbahu (four-armed), Dinabandhu (friend of the destitute), Mahatma (great soul), Bhaktavatsala (tender and loving to devotees); Sanjiivananagaharta (one who lifted the Sanjiivani mountain), Shuchi (pure), Vagmi (eloquent), Dridhavrata (firm in his vows).

contentBlocks.original:
कालनेमिप्रमथनो हरिमर्कटमर्कटः ।
दान्तः शान्तः प्रसन्नात्मा शतकण्ठमदापहृत् ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kālanēmipramathanō harimarkaṭamarkaṭaḥ ।
dāntaḥ śāntaḥ prasannātmā śatakaṇṭhamadāpahṛt ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kalanemipramathana (destroyer of the demon Kalanemi), Hari Markata Markata (the monkey devoted to Vishnu — monkey of monkeys); Danta (self-controlled), Shanta (peaceful), Prasannatma (serene in spirit), Shatakaonthamadapahrit (remover of Ravana's hundredfold pride).

contentBlocks.original:
योगी रामकथालोलः सीतान्वेषणपण्डितः ।
वज्रदंष्ट्रो वज्रनखो रुद्रवीर्यसमुद्भवः ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgī rāmakathālōlaḥ sītānvēṣaṇapaṇḍitaḥ ।
vajradaṃṣṭrō vajranakhō rudravīryasamudbhavaḥ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Yogi (yogi), Ramakathaloala (deeply absorbed in hearing Rama's story), Sitanveshanapandita (expert in searching for Sita); Vajradamshtra (thunderbolt-fanged), Vajranakha (thunderbolt-nailed), Rudraviiryasamudbhava (born of the valor of Rudra).

Indrajitprahitamogha Brahmastravinivaaraka (one who neutralized the infallible Brahmastra unleashed by Indrajit); Parthadhvajaagrasanvasiì (one who dwells on Arjuna's battle-banner), Sharapañjarabhèdaka (one who pierces through the cage of arrows).

Dashbahu (ten-armed), Lokapuíjya (worshipped by all the worlds), Jambavatpritivardhana (one who deepened Jambavan's joy and faith); Sitasameta Shriiramapaadasevaaadhurandhara (foremost servant of the lotus feet of Lord Rama together with Sita Devi).

contentBlocks.original:
इत्येवं श्रीहनुमतो नाम्नामष्टोत्तरं शतम् ।
यः पठेच्छृणुयान्नित्यं सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात् ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ityēvaṃ śrīhanumatō nāmnāmaṣṭōttaraṃ śatam ।
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyānnityaṃ sarvānkāmānavāpnuyāt ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus these 108 names of Sri Hanuman have been recited. Whoever reads or hears this Ashtottara Sata Naama daily with devotion shall attain the fulfilment of all desired objects.
